q: The coefficient of $x^2$ in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$, $x \neq 0$, is :
question: The coefficient of $x^2$ in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$, $x \neq 0$, is :
solution:
The general term in the expansion of $\left(2x^2 + \dfrac{1}{x}\right)^{10}$ is given by:

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} (2x^2)^{10-r} \left(\dfrac{1}{x}\right)^r$

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} 2^{10-r} x^{20-2r} x^{-r}$

$T_{r+1} = ^{10}C_{r} 2^{10-r} x^{20-3r}$

For the coefficient of $x^2$, we equate the power of $x$ to $2$:

$20 - 3r = 2$

$3r = 18 \Rightarrow r = 6$

Substituting $r = 6$ in the coefficient part, we get:

Coefficient $= ^{10}C_{6} 2^{10-6}$

$= ^{10}C_{4} 2^4$

$= \dfrac{10 \times 9 \times 8 \times 7}{4 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} \times 16$

$= 210 \times 16$

$= 3360$

Answer: $3360$

solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \left(\mathrm{x}^2+2 \mathrm{x}\right)(12-\mathrm{k})=2 \\ & \lambda \mathrm{x}^2+2 \lambda \mathrm{x}-2=0 \quad \mathrm{k} \neq 12 \text { Let } 12-\mathrm{k}=\lambda \\ & \mathrm{D}=0 \\ & 4 \lambda^2+8 \lambda=0 \\ & \lambda=0 \text { or } \lambda=-2 \\ & \Rightarrow 12-\mathrm{k}=-2 \\ & \mathrm{k}=14
\end{aligned}$
$\text { So } P\left(k, \frac{k}{2}\right)=(14,7)$
$\mathrm{d}=\left|\frac{3 \times 14+4 \times 7+5}{5}\right|=15$

question: If $|z-4| < |z-2|$, its solution is given by
solution:
Given $|z-4| < |z-2|$ Let $z=x+$ iy
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow \mid(x-4)+i y)| < |(x-2)+i y \mid \Rightarrow(x-4)^2+y^2 < (x-2)^2+y^2 \\

\end{aligned}
$$

question: $\frac{d^2 x}{d y^2}$ equals

$\begin{aligned} & (2 y-5) \frac{d y}{d x}=-3 \\ & (2 y-5) d y=-3 d x \\ & 2 \cdot \frac{y^2}{2}-5 y=-3 x+\lambda\end{aligned}$

$\left(y-\frac{5}{2}\right)^2=-3\left(x-\frac{3}{4}\right)$

$\because 2 x+3 y=9$

solution:
Given that α be the remainder when 222022+202222 is divided by 3 and β be the remainder when the same is divided by 7.

⇒222022+202222=21+12022+202222.

Here 202222 is divisible by 3 as 2022 is divisible by 3.

So on expanding 21+12022, we get

⇒21+12022=C02022212022+C12022212021+......+C2022202212022

=332021×72022+C12022×32020×72021+......+1

=3k1+1

In this case the remainder is 1

Hence, α=1

Now, ⇒222022+202222=21+12022+2023-122

Take 2023-122

⇒2023-122=C022202322-C122202321+......+C2222-122

=7C02272128922-C12272028921+......+1

=7k2+1

⇒21+12022+2023-122=7k1+1+7k2+1

=7μ+2

⇒β=2.

Hence, α2+β2=12+22=5.

Therefore, the required answer is 5.

q: Light is incident from a medium into air at two possible angles of incidence (A) $20^{\circ}$ and (B) $40^{\circ}$. In the medium light travels $3.0 \mathrm{~cm}$ in $0.2 \mathrm{~ns}$. The ray will :
question: Light is incident from a medium into air at two possible angles of incidence (A) $20^{\circ}$ and (B) $40^{\circ}$. In the medium light travels $3.0 \mathrm{~cm}$ in $0.2 \mathrm{~ns}$. The ray will :
options: suffer total internal reflection in both cases (A) and (B)
suffer total internal reflection in case (B) only
have partial reflection and partial transmission in case (B)
solution:
Velocity of light in medium
$$
\mathrm{V}_{\text {med }}=\frac{3 \mathrm{~cm}}{0.2 \mathrm{~ns}}=\frac{3 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}}{0.2 \times 10^{-9} \mathrm{~s}}=1.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
$$
Refractive index of the medium
$$
\mu=\frac{V_{\text {air }}}{V_{\text {med }}}=\frac{3 \times 10^8}{1.5}=2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}
$$
As $\mu=\frac{1}{\sin C}$
$$
\therefore \sin \mathrm{C}=\frac{1}{\mu}=\frac{1}{2}=30^{\circ}
$$
Condition of TIR is angle of incidence i must be greater than critical angle. Hence ray will suffer TIR in case of $(B)\left(i=40^{\circ}>30^{\circ}\right)$ only.

question: A convex lens of focal length 30 cm is placed in contact with a concave lens of focal length 20 cm. An object is placed at 20 cm to the left of this lens system. The distance of the image from the lens in cm is ________

$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{f}=\frac{1}{f_1}+\frac{1}{f_2} \\ & =\frac{1}{30}+\frac{1}{-20}=\frac{2-3}{60}=-\frac{1}{60} \\ & f=-60 \mathrm{~cm}
\end{aligned}$

q: A certain gas is isothermally compressed to $\left(\dfrac{1}{3}\right)^{rd}$ of its initial volume ($V_o = 3$ litre) by applying required pressure. If the bulk modulus of the gas is $3 \times 10^5$ N/m$^2$, the magnitude of work done on the gas is _______ J.
question: A certain gas is isothermally compressed to $\left(\dfrac{1}{3}\right)^{rd}$ of its initial volume ($V_o = 3$ litre) by applying required pressure. If the bulk modulus of the gas is $3 \times 10^5$ N/m$^2$, the magnitude of work done on the gas is _______ J.
solution:
For an isothermal process, the isothermal bulk modulus equals the gas pressure:
$B_T = -V\dfrac{dP}{dV}$

Since $PV = \text{constant}$, differentiating gives $P\,dV + V\,dP = 0$, so $\dfrac{dP}{dV} = -\dfrac{P}{V}$, which yields $B_T = P$.

Therefore, the initial pressure is:
$P_o = 3 \times 10^5\text{ N/m}^2$

Volumes:
$V_o = 3\text{ L} = 3 \times 10^{-3}\text{ m}^3$
$V_f = \dfrac{1}{3}V_o = 10^{-3}\text{ m}^3$

Work done on the gas during an isothermal compression:
$W_{\text{on}} = -W_{\text{by}} = -P_o V_o \ln\left(\dfrac{V_f}{V_o}\right) = P_o V_o \ln\left(\dfrac{V_o}{V_f}\right)$

$W_{\text{on}} = (3 \times 10^5)(3 \times 10^{-3})\ln 3$

$W_{\text{on}} = 900 \ln 3\text{ J}$

Using $\ln 3 \approx 1.0986$:
$W_{\text{on}} \approx 900 \times 1.0986 \approx 988.74\text{ J}$

Rounded to the nearest integer:
$W_{\text{on}} \approx 989\text{ J}$

Hence, the work done on the gas is approximately $989\text{ J}$.

q: A metal string $A$ is suspended from a rigid support and its free end is attached to a block of mass $M$. Second block having mass $2M$ is suspended at the bottom of the first block using a string $B$. The area of cross sections of strings $A$ and $B$ are same. The ratio of lengths of strings of $A$ to $B$ is $2$ and the ratio of their Young's moduli $(Y_A/Y_B)$ is $0.5$. The ratio of elongations in $A$ to $B$ is ______.
question: A metal string $A$ is suspended from a rigid support and its free end is attached to a block of mass $M$. Second block having mass $2M$ is suspended at the bottom of the first block using a string $B$. The area of cross sections of strings $A$ and $B$ are same. The ratio of lengths of strings of $A$ to $B$ is $2$ and the ratio of their Young's moduli $(Y_A/Y_B)$ is $0.5$. The ratio of elongations in $A$ to $B$ is ______.
solution:
Tension in string $B$ is due to the block of mass $2M$:

$T_B = 2Mg$

Tension in string $A$ is due to both blocks of mass $M$ and $2M$:

$T_A = Mg + 2Mg = 3Mg$

The elongation in a string is given by Hooke's law as $\Delta L = \dfrac{TL}{AY}$.

The ratio of elongations in string $A$ to string $B$ is:

$\dfrac{\Delta L_A}{\Delta L_B} = \left(\dfrac{T_A}{T_B}\right) \left(\dfrac{L_A}{L_B}\right) \left(\dfrac{A_B}{A_A}\right) \left(\dfrac{Y_B}{Y_A}\right)$

Given that $\dfrac{L_A}{L_B} = 2$, $\dfrac{A_B}{A_A} = 1$, and $\dfrac{Y_A}{Y_B} = 0.5 \Rightarrow \dfrac{Y_B}{Y_A} = 2$.

Substituting the values into the ratio equation:

$\dfrac{\Delta L_A}{\Delta L_B} = \left(\dfrac{3Mg}{2Mg}\right) \times (2) \times (1) \times (2)$

$\dfrac{\Delta L_A}{\Delta L_B} = \dfrac{3}{2} \times 4 = 6$

Answer: $6$

subject: Physics
q: The percentage error in the calculated volume of a sphere, if there is $2\%$ error in its diameter measurement, is __________.
question: The percentage error in the calculated volume of a sphere, if there is $2\%$ error in its diameter measurement, is __________.
solution:
The volume of a sphere in terms of its diameter $D$ is given by $V = \dfrac{\pi}{6}D^3$

Taking the natural logarithm on both sides, we get $\ln V = \ln\left(\dfrac{\pi}{6}\right) + 3 \ln D$

Differentiating for small errors, we obtain $\dfrac{\Delta V}{V} = 3 \dfrac{\Delta D}{D}$

Multiplying by $100$ to find the percentage error, $\dfrac{\Delta V}{V} \times 100 = 3 \left(\dfrac{\Delta D}{D} \times 100\right)$

Given that the percentage error in the diameter is $2\%$, we have $\dfrac{\Delta D}{D} \times 100 = 2$

Therefore, the percentage error in the volume is $3 \times 2 = 6\%$

Answer: $6$

q: Which of the following is least ionic?
question: Which of the following is least ionic?
solution:
In inorganic chemistry, Fajans' rules, formulated by Kazimierz Fajans in 1923, are used to predict whether a chemical bond will be covalent or ionic, and depend on the charge on the cation and the relative sizes of the cation and anion. They can be summarised in the following table:


$\begin{array}{|l|l|} \hline \text{Ionic } & \text{Covalent } \\ \hline \text{Low positive charge on cation} & \text{High positive charge on cation} \\ \hline \text{Large cation} & \text{Small cation} \\ \hline \text{Small anion} & \text{Large anion} \\ \hline \end{array}$

In the given case all the anions are same in all the given compounds. So we need to think about cations.

The ionic radius of Ag+ is less than that of Cl− but their charges are equal. Hence, AgCl is a least ionic compound among the given options.

AgCl<CoCl2<BaCl2<KCl (ionic character)

q: If 5 moles of BaCl2 is mixed with 2 moles of Na3PO4, the maximum number of moles of Ba3PO42formed is _________ (Nearest integer)
question: If 5 moles of BaCl2 is mixed with 2 moles of Na3PO4, the maximum number of moles of Ba3PO42formed is _________ (Nearest integer)
solution:
The balanced chemical equation for the reaction between BaCl2 and Na3PO4 is:

3BaCl2 + 2Na3PO4 → Ba3(PO4)2 + 6NaCl

5 moles of barium chloride will require 5×23=3.3 moles of sodium phosphate.

Here, sodium phosphate is the limiting reagent.
2 moles of sodium phosphate produce 1 mole of barium phosphate.

Thus,2 moles of sodium phosphate will produce 1 mole of barium phosphate.

Hence, the maximum no. of moles of Ba3(PO4)2 that can be formed is one mole.

q: 200 cc of $x \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$ potassium dichromate is required to oxidise 750 cc of 0.6 M Mohr's salt solution in acidic medium. Here $x=$ $\_\_\_\_$.
question: 200 cc of $x \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{M}$ potassium dichromate is required to oxidise 750 cc of 0.6 M Mohr's salt solution in acidic medium. Here $x=$ $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
Mohr's salt ($FeSO_4 \cdot (NH_4)_2SO_4 \cdot 6H_2O$) is a source of $Fe^{2+}$ ions.
The redox reaction with potassium dichromate in acidic medium is:
$6Fe^{2+} + Cr_2O_7^{2-} + 14H^+ \to 6Fe^{3+} + 2Cr^{3+} + 7H_2O$.
Moles of $Fe^{2+}$ from Mohr's salt solution:
$n(Fe^{2+}) = 0.6 \text{ M} \times 0.750 \text{ L} = 0.45 \text{ mol}$.
From the stoichiometry, 6 moles of $Fe^{2+}$ require 1 mole of $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$.
Moles of $Cr_2O_7^{2-}$ required:
$n(Cr_2O_7^{2-}) = \frac{0.45}{6} = 0.075 \text{ mol}$.
Molarity of potassium dichromate:
$M = \frac{0.075 \text{ mol}}{0.200 \text{ L}} = 0.375 \text{ M} = 375 \times 10^{-3} \text{ M}$.
Therefore, $x = 375$.

solution:
Statement A: In $NH_3$, the orbital dipole due to the lone pair is in the same direction as the resultant dipole moment of the $N-H$ bonds. In $NF_3$, the orbital dipole is in the opposite direction to the resultant dipole moment of the $N-F$ bonds. Thus, dipole moment of $NH_3$ ($1.47\ D$) is higher than $NF_3$ ($0.23\ D$). Statement A is incorrect.
Statement B: $BeH_2$ is a linear molecule ($sp$ hybridization). The two $Be-H$ bond dipoles are equal and opposite, so they cancel each other out, making the net dipole moment zero. Statement B is correct.
Statement C: $O_2^{2-}$ has 18 electrons. Its bond order is $\frac{10-8}{2} = 1$. $F_2$ also has 18 electrons and its bond order is 1. Statement C is correct.
Statement D: In the Lewis structure of ozone ($O_3$), the central oxygen atom forms one double bond and one single bond and has one lone pair. Formal charge = $V - L - \frac{B}{2} = 6 - 2 - \frac{6}{2} = +1$. Statement D is incorrect.
Statement E: $NO_2$ is an odd-electron molecule (17 valence electrons). The nitrogen atom has only 7 electrons in its valence shell, so it does not satisfy the octet rule. Statement E is incorrect.
Therefore, only statements B and C are correct.
